title: StaticCheckResult
description: Audit artifact for Phase B.6 of Step 8 codegen: native static-check gate. Runs the SUT stack's own type-checker (pyright for Python stacks; tsc for JS/TS stacks) against the qtea-generated test files and their direct imports. Persisted to `artifacts/step08/static-check-result.json` on every run, including skipped cases (no checker for the stack, missing tool with no auto-install path, no in-scope files). Caller uses `in_scope_errors > 0` post-fixer to decide whether to fail Step 8.

tool.description: Static-check tool that was selected for the stack: `pyright` for Python, `tsc` for JS/TS. `null` when the stack has no dispatch entry (java/robot/ruby/go).

stack.description: Detected test stack (matches the framework enum in `tbd-index.schema.json`).

ran.description: True iff the static-checker was actually invoked (subprocess executed) and parsed output was attempted. False when the phase short-circuited for any reason — `skipped_reason` then carries the cause.

skipped_reason.description: Populated when `ran=false`. Common values: `"no checker for framework=..."` (stack outside v1 dispatch), `"no auto-install path for package_manager=..."` (tool absent and no install argv known), `"no Python files in qteaouched set"` / `"no JS/TS files in qteaouched set"` (post-filter scope is empty), `"env_skip"` (QTEA_SKIP_STATIC_CHECK=1), `"flag_skip"` (--no-static-check), `"checker exit N: <stderr head>"` (tool launched but emitted no parseable output). Null when `ran=true`.

duration_s.description: Wall-clock seconds from phase entry to result construction. Includes any auto-install subprocess time.

exit_code.description: Exit code from the static-checker subprocess. 0 when ran=false. Pyright exits non-zero on type errors; tsc exits 1 on type errors and 2 on bad CLI usage.

in_scope_errors.description: Count of error-severity violations whose file is in the qteaouched set after post-parse filtering. This is the count the caller uses to decide whether to invoke the fixer / fail the step.

out_of_scope_errors.description: Count of error-severity violations the checker reported on files OUTSIDE the qteaouched set (e.g. transitively-imported user app source). Retained in `violations[]` with severity="out_of_scope" for audit but never fed to the fixer.

autofix_attempted.description: True iff `codegen-violation-fixer` was invoked on the in-scope violations. Mirrors Phase B.5's single-retry policy: at most one fixer pass per step run.

post_fix_errors.description: In-scope error count after the fixer pass and a re-run of the checker. Only meaningful when `autofix_attempted=true`. Non-zero here triggers `StepResult(success=False)` and escalates to the global MAX_ATTEMPTS=2 step retry.

violations.description: Every violation parsed from the checker output, in-scope and out-of-scope together. The in-scope subset is identified by `severity != "out_of_scope"`. Capped at 50 entries in the fixer prompt but the artifact persists the full list.

rule.description: Single value `"type-error"` for everything emitted by Phase B.6. Distinguishes these rows from `xpath` / `hard-wait` / etc. produced by other Step 8 phases.

file.description: SUT-relative posix path of the file the checker flagged.

line.description: 1-based line number (pyright JSON is 0-based; the parser normalises).

snippet.description: The checker's message, suffixed with its rule code in brackets when available (e.g. `"Cannot access attribute \"X\" for class \"Y\" [reportAttributeAccessIssue]"` / `"Cannot find name 'foo'. [TS2304]"`). Capped at 280 chars.

severity.description: `"error"` for in-scope rows; `"out_of_scope"` for rows whose file is outside the qteaouched set. The fixer only sees `"error"` rows. Warnings from the checker are dropped at parse time (they produce noise the fixer can't act on safely).
